title: Is it Well with the Child? by Christina Rossetti — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
SAFE where I cannot die yet,
   Safe where I hope to lie too,
Safe from the fume and the fret;
   You, and you,
   Whom I never forget.
Safe from the frost and the snow,
   Safe from the storm and the sun,
Safe where the seeds wait to grow
   One by one,
   And to come back in blow.
meaning: "Is it Well with the Child?" by Christina Rossetti is a deep poem consisting of 10 lines. The poet explores themes of deep through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "SAFE where I cannot die yet,    Safe where I hope to lie too,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Christina Rossetti's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
